description_long: This is a traditional pitchfork featuring a long, straight handle with a muted greenish hue, likely made of wood or metal. The handle is slender and tapers slightly towards the top, providing a comfortable grip for two-handed use. At the base, the pitchfork has a robust metal head with four evenly spaced, straight tines that are slightly pointed for effective penetration into soil or loose materials. The tines are joined by a solid crossbar, ensuring structural integrity and strength during use. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, making it suitable for practical tasks such as moving hay, compost, or debris in urban gardens, parks, or maintenance areas. The surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no visible signs of rust or significant wear.
description: A classic pitchfork with a long greenish handle and four sturdy metal tines, designed for practical use in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the handle and all four tines clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the handle and the narrow alignment of the tines.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the handle and all four tines visible from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the handle and the tines aligned edge-on.
